text: Newsletter of U3A Redlands District Inc.
Great News - The early bird catches the discount!
Thank you to our members for your support and patience over the past challenging
months. In acknowledgement, the Management Committee is offering a reduced
membership fee to those who renew or apply during the month of January
2021 only. Please check the newsletter, your emails and the website for details of
payment options. If you are wondering, there hasn’t been a financial windfall for the
organisation, but a combination of prudent spending by our Treasurer, Janese Lowe
and some unanticipated government support, has put us in a position that we can offer
We are asking renewing members and new members to WAIT UNTIL 1 JANUARY
2021 before paying your 2021 membership fee, so that you pay the reduced annual
From 1 January we encourage members to pay online through our webpage,
button and the “Pay Online” link. More details will follow and are on our website.
Also, from 19 January the Office will be open for four days to receive over the counter
payments, and for assistance with online enrolments. Information will also be available
Please be aware that we will be maintaining appropriate COVID safety practices in the
Office so you may be asked to wait.
With no certainty of the health situation in January next year, it has been decided to
cancel the annual Enrolment/Sign-On Day for the reason that our usual format e.g. in
2020 at RPAC, would not guarantee a COVID safe environment in 2021.
The Management Committee is investigating a suitable venue for another Members
Welcome Event to be held in February 2021.
Again, check the newsletter, your emails, the website and mobile texts for details.

MyU3A is our new U3A administration system and we hope the below Tutorials will help you to

the password that was emailed to you in July. Follow the Forgot Password/ID instructions below
if you cannot find your password and one will be forwarded to you. Please follow the steps to
update your profile with correct year of birth and emergency contact details.

Many Australians know very little about our nearest neighbour. Most media articles tend
to concentrate on the occasional sensationalism and ignore almost everything else.
Papua New Guinea has been and will become very important to future Australians.
Unfortunately, the knowledge of our shared history and PNG's vibrant culture is

The Writing for Pleasure group addresses the times we live in (maybe). Enjoy....
ZYPTON’S INVASION Written by Ruth Cooney
There was something primeval about the throb of the drums. The sound echoed around the
bayside suburb, an alien insistent noise. Not a discordant Saturday arvo garage band practice.
This was a single, deep-throated reverberation, slow then frantically fast peaks rolling over
rooftops. Zypton's seventh finger of his third hand pressed the lever back up, slowing the beat
of the throbbing drums. His deep purple lips stretched into a smile. Zeltonians didn't smile
often. A deep robotic sound tumbled out of his mouth. "We will see how long these Earthlings
can withstand the life-sucking reverberations of our terminator. No longer than the Martians
did, I will venture." Another purple skinned Zeltonian spoke. "We will have them shattering to
pieces in no time. Give them a stronger blast, Captain!" As Zypton pushed the lever again,
making the drums throb faster and louder, the Zeltonian calculated that the Earthlings would
start to fall within 30 earth minutes.
Zypton and Zimmtah sat in their spaceship just 500 metres above the bayside suburb but
these backward humans couldn't see their aircraft. An invisible shield blanketed the ship. The
Earthlings had no idea where the sound came from. That noise would soon suck the life from
these intensely stupid Earthlings and he, Zypton would take over the Earth and set up his own
home base. He was a leader! For over 30 Earth minutes, Zypton pushed and pulled the lever,
the haunting drumbeats flooding the suburb below. Zypton became agitated. He didn't observe
any Earthlings writhing in agony or lying dead.
As a matter of fact, he didn't observe any Earthlings at all. There were no human beings
anywhere on the ground beneath the spaceship, dead or alive. Zypton switched off the
Terminator. Zimmtah sidled up to his captain's chair. "What is wrong, Captain?" "There are no
Earthlings down there, Zimmtah. Earth has already been exterminated, probably with high
powered radiation waves. Tie in, Zimmtah.
We move on to Jupiter!" Little did Zypton know that the whole population of that bayside
suburb couldn't hear those throbbing drums. They had earpieces in their ears listening to their
Prime Minister telling them that Stage Four Lockdown would come into effect from midnight
Sunday. The coronavirus was getting away from them. The Prime Minister said, "It could mean
the end of Earth as we know it."
Not even a global pandemic can stop the U3A Ukulele Band. Since we were unable to
meet in our hall, our fearless leader Laurie Dyer taught us to use the Zoom platform
so we could pick up our ukulele each week and play together. We did appreciate not
missing out on our joyful group. On returning to the hall last month we were restricted
to 35 players so our other awesome leader Bob Foster manned the hall and coordinated
a roster system so each player could have a turn to attend. We can now have 50
players of our large group and Tuesdays are looking almost normal again. Our first
concert for this year will be at Salford Waters Retirement Estate at the end of this
Hope all the Dad’s and Grandfathers in our CCC group had a good day. As we did not
have a U3A caravan get away planned Pauline and I with husband’s Gary and John took
advantage of the time of year for weather. We set off with our roof top tents [no
caravans for this trip] and headed towards Birdsville Track going across the Tirari
Desert and Sturt Stony Desert and coming back up the Strzelecki desert. It was quite
an experience and we were lucky with the weather. We only had one sandstorm to
contend with which made it impossible to see the road ahead. The sandstorm did stop
us driving but thank goodness it did not last long.

An example of a Technical Gem from this month’s free booklet:
As the Pass It On Network grows, so will the choice of activities. Our goal is to discover
innovative, inexpensive programs, projects and ideas that generate and support active,
productive, positive aging wherever they have been developed around the world, and to
“pass them on” through our network for sharing and adapting.
We are both teachers and learners all our life. Here, we bring you programs and information on
teaching and learning that expand access and respond to the needs and interests of older adults.
There’s no better way to gain personal support for positive aging than with our peers who are
sharing their experience. Here, you will find innovative guides to form mutual support groups
*** Subscribe for your email copy at u3aonline.org.au
The lumberjack’s favourite part about computers is logging in. 
The space program is awfully expensive as the cost is astronomical. 
The stadium heated up after the game because all the fans left. 
The chemistry student failed as he was out of his element. 
My son asked what our IP address was. I pointed to the toilet. 

Another year overseen by the current Management Committee is nearly over.
Our Annual General Meeting for 2020 is scheduled for Monday 30 November at
9.30 am in the Donald Simpson Centre.
We are seeking a volunteer to act as Secretary to complete our Management
Committee and to contribute towards the ongoing success of U3A Redlands District
Contact one of the current Executive members for further information:
